methods.description: This method involves the experimental verification of the bandgaps and vibration attenuation performance of a typical lattice plate and a SPLCRR plate. The experiment aims to validate the numerical predictions made using FEM by comparing the measured vibration suppression ranges with the predicted bandgaps.

steps.description: The specimens contain 7 × 7 units (360 mm × 360 mm) with the same unit geometry and material parameters as in the FEM in Section 3.1.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare specimens with specified geometry and material parameters

steps.description: Two end sides of the specimen are simply supported by foam materials to mimic the simply supported boundary condition.
steps.searchable_summary: set up boundary conditions using foam supports

steps.description: The vibration transmission spectra of the sandwich plates specimen were obtained by collecting the impact hammer (Brüel and Kjaer, 8206-002) of the exciting point and the vibration acceleration (Brüel and Kjaer, 4507) responses of the acquisition point with data acquisition (Brüel and Kjaer, 3040).
steps.searchable_summary: collect vibration transmission spectra using impact hammer and accelerometer

steps.description: It was observed that the measured vibration suppression ranges exhibited a strong agreement with both the predicted bandgaps and the previous FEM simulation.
steps.searchable_summary: compare measured vibration suppression with predicted bandgaps

methods.description: This method involves using the Finite Element Method (FEM) to calculate the dispersion relations and investigate the bandgaps of the proposed SPLCRR plate. The method uses the Solid Mechanics Module of COMSOL to derive the dispersion curves and identify the band structure of the plate.

steps.description: A series of calculations of dispersion relations are conducted with the FEM based on Solid Mechanics Module of the COMSOL.
steps.searchable_summary: perform FEM calculations for dispersion relations using COMSOL

steps.description: Based on the periodicity of the proposed plate, we assumed that in both the x and y directions, a single unit cell was considered.
steps.searchable_summary: model single unit cell based on periodicity

steps.description: By modulating the wave vector k within the first irreducible Brillouin zone, derive the dispersion curves of the unit cell with respect to its wave number and frequency.
steps.searchable_summary: derive dispersion curves by modulating wave vector

steps.description: To further verify the bandgap properties of the SPLCRR, the vibration attenuation characteristics of this plate were calculated by FEM.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate vibration attenuation characteristics using FEM
